subject: Estimating delay diagnostic knowledge base
description: Collaborative knowledge base for diagnosing why costing and quotation processes are late and for proposing corrective actions through linked causes, effects, solutions, and metrics.

data.subject: Incomplete RFQ package at intake
data.description: Costing starts without complete drawings, BOM, confirmed volumes, or fixed logistics assumptions. This creates clarification loops and rework.
symptoms: Repeated questions back to sales or customer
Several costing file revisions before first release
Late start despite RFQ officially received
questions: Was the RFQ package complete on day 0?
Which mandatory inputs were missing?
Did the team start anyway before receiving all inputs?
evidence_to_collect: Date when each missing item was received
root_cause_hypotheses: Sales not trained on mandatory costing inputs
Pressure to start early with unstable assumptions
actions: Block detailed costing until minimum inputs are present
Use provisional quote mode for incomplete cases
metrics: Lead time from RFQ receipt to usable package

collaboration.workshop_prompt: Review the last 10 late RFQs and list which inputs were missing at intake.

data.subject: Insufficient costing capacity
data.description: The number of requests exceeds the real capacity of the costing team. Backlog grows and priorities collide.
symptoms: Frequent overtime with no lead-time improvement
questions: How many RFQs arrive per week versus completed per week?
What is the current backlog in days or RFQs?
Which tasks consume the most analyst time?
evidence_to_collect: Time spent per RFQ by complexity level
root_cause_hypotheses: Too many low-probability RFQs treated like priority jobs
actions: Segment RFQs by strategic value and complexity
Create fast lane for simple quotes
Add backup resources or cross-train staff

collaboration.workshop_prompt: Classify the current RFQ backlog by complexity, customer importance, probability, and deadline.

data.subject: Late supplier quotation
data.description: Costing waits for supplier prices, material prices, or external process data. Repeated follow-ups block the estimate.
symptoms: Quote waits on purchasing for several days
Same supplier is repeatedly a bottleneck
questions: What percentage of quote lead time is spent waiting for suppliers?
Which supplier categories respond late most often?
Can a provisional should-cost be used while waiting?
evidence_to_collect: Supplier RFQ issue date vs supplier reply date
root_cause_hypotheses: RFQ package sent to suppliers incomplete
actions: Build should-cost library for recurrent components
metrics: % quote files blocked by supplier delay

effect_links.relation_label: reduced by should-cost and supplier SLA

collaboration.decision_needed: Use provisional costing or escalate supplier delay
collaboration.workshop_prompt: Map the top 20 bought parts that most often block quotation timing.

data.subject: Costing tool too heavy or poorly integrated
data.description: Analysts lose time through re-entry, file navigation, or an overly complex Excel model.
questions: How much time is spent on calculation versus data entry?
Which data must be entered manually more than once?
Can standard assumptions be preloaded?
evidence_to_collect: Process timing study of one quote
Count of tools or files opened per quote
root_cause_hypotheses: ERP, PLM, and costing are not connected
actions: Create one quote package folder or digital form
metrics: Manual data entry time per quote
Number of files used per quote

collaboration.decision_needed: Approve template redesign or automation effort
collaboration.workshop_prompt: Shadow one analyst during a full quote and identify all non-value-added tool steps.

data.subject: Costing rework
data.description: The file is recalculated several times because of unstable inputs, late-corrected assumptions, or multiple versions.
symptoms: More than two major quote revisions
questions: How many revisions happened before release?
Which assumptions changed after the first build?
Could a version freeze have prevented this?
actions: Freeze input version before detailed costing
Use change log with impact assessment
Escalate if assumptions change after gate

collaboration.decision_needed: Define version freeze and exception rule
collaboration.workshop_prompt: Review the last 5 quotes with more than 2 revisions and identify the late-changing assumptions.

data.subject: Shifting priorities and permanent urgencies
data.description: Work is interrupted by more urgent requests. Analysts start several quotes without properly finishing one.
symptoms: Large number of open quotes with slow completion
Internal conflict on what is really urgent
questions: Who decides quote priority today?
How many times was the work sequence changed this week?
What percentage of urgent quotes were truly strategic?
actions: Separate fast lane and strategic lane

collaboration.decision_needed: Set a formal quote prioritization mechanism
collaboration.workshop_prompt: Rank current open quotes by probability, margin potential, customer criticality, and due date.

data.subject: Detailed costing blocked
data.description: The team cannot complete the estimate because reliable cost data is missing on one or more key items.
questions: Which cost line is still uncertain?
Can a bounded estimate be used?
Who owns the missing data?
evidence_to_collect: Open issues list by cost line
Range estimate for uncertain cost lines
Owner and due date per open issue
actions: Use bounded assumptions with risk note
Force owner assignment on each open issue
metrics: Open critical assumptions at release review
Average time to close blocking issue

collaboration.decision_needed: Approve bounded assumption or wait for missing data
collaboration.workshop_prompt: List the three cost lines that most often block quote completion and define default fallback assumptions.

data.subject: Quote released late
data.description: The customer or sales team receives the quote after the expected date, with high commercial and operational risk.
symptoms: Lower win probability or rushed poor-quality release
questions: How late was the quote compared with the committed date?
Was quality also degraded to catch up?
What was the most time-consuming stage?
evidence_to_collect: Committed due date vs release date
Commercial impact or lost opportunity note
root_cause_hypotheses: Combination of upstream causes not treated early
actions: Review late quotes weekly with root-cause Pareto
Assign single owner for due-date commitment

collaboration.decision_needed: Define weekly late-quote review with action ownership
collaboration.workshop_prompt: For each late quote this month, mark the stage where time was lost and the preventable root cause.

data.subject: Mandatory RFQ intake gate
data.description: Define a minimum set of inputs without which detailed costing cannot start.
questions: Which fields are mandatory for detailed costing?
actions: Allow only bounded budgetary quote without full inputs

collaboration.workshop_prompt: Agree the minimum RFQ dataset required for detailed costing.

data.subject: Segmentation of quote flow
data.description: Separate simple, strategic, complex, and exploratory quotes so everything does not follow the same path.
questions: Which quote types really need full detailed costing?
actions: Set standard lead times per class

collaboration.workshop_prompt: Create 3 to 4 quote classes with expected depth and lead time.

data.subject: Should-cost plus supplier SLA
data.description: Create a backup price basis and set a target supplier response time.
questions: Which bought parts most often block the quote?
actions: Create should-cost for top recurring parts

collaboration.decision_needed: Choose supplier categories for should-cost first
collaboration.workshop_prompt: Prioritize the top purchased items that create the biggest quoting delays.

data.subject: Simplify costing model
data.description: Reduce re-entry, unnecessary tabs, and repeated assumptions.
questions: Which manual steps can be removed first?

collaboration.workshop_prompt: Map all manual steps in the current model and remove non-value-added ones.

data.subject: Quote priority governance
data.description: Implement an explicit prioritization rule and limit work in progress.
questions: Who owns final priority decision?
evidence_to_collect: Current open RFQ list with strategic score

collaboration.decision_needed: Approve scoring model and WIP limits
collaboration.workshop_prompt: Define 3 to 5 criteria that make a quote truly urgent.

data.subject: Quote OTD
data.description: Percentage of quotes released on or before the committed date.
questions: What is the current OTD by quote class?

collaboration.decision_needed: Set target OTD and review cadence
collaboration.workshop_prompt: Agree what committed date should be counted when several dates exist.
